decision_rule: Adopt Astral-like structure, behavior, metadata, and consumer surface wherever upstream bytes or bounded project support make it truthful; exclude or defer only producer surfaces and dependencies unavailable without a separate build.

selections.decision: Install pip only from the wheel already carried by the official package; do not acquire a replacement wheel and do not change the native producer topology.
selections.release_gate: deterministic package-only bootstrap and relocation qualification

selections.decision: Provide Astral-like pip, pip3, and pip3.14 command surfaces only through relocation-safe project wrappers; python -m pip remains the normative fallback.
selections.release_gate: E3-X5 wrapper relocation and shell-contract experiment

selections.decision: Provide a versioned project-supported CA payload outside the immutable Python install tree through DATA_ROOT; preserve caller override and independent update/rollback.
selections.release_gate: E3-X9 provider, license, update, rollback, and corruption tests

selections.decision: Provide a versioned project-supported timezone payload outside the immutable Python install tree through DATA_ROOT; do not write updates into the archive-derived prefix.
selections.release_gate: E3-X9 provider, license, update, rollback, and corruption tests

selections.decision: Do not add a non-Astral runtime-only archive flavor. The canonical user artifact is Astral install_only derived from full.
selections.reason: A separate runtime-only flavor is not required for Astral archive parity and would create a project-specific product class.

selections.decision: Retain upstream headers, shared libpython, Python pkg-config, and producer metadata. Apply the selected profile M minimal consumer/on-device-toolchain overlay inside the normal install tree; portable wheel ELF cleanup is a separate explicit product boundary.
selections.non_claim: No static relinking, custom built-ins, dependency rebuild support, cross-build NDK SDK, or claim that ordinary raw native wheels are portable without explicit ELF normalization.

selections.decision: Do not bundle an NDK, sysroot, producer object tree, or complete cross toolchain in upstream-thin.
selections.reason: The official embedding package does not contain the complete producer material required for an Astral-style relinking SDK.

selections.decision: Ship the bounded subprocess surface already evidenced with sys.executable child re-entry; document unavailable or Android-sensitive operations explicitly.
Support creation of new venvs before or after base installation movement only within the qualified cases; require recreation of pre-existing venvs after base relocation.
selections.non_claim: Arbitrary relocation of existing venvs is unsupported.

selections.decision: Do not claim general multiprocessing support in the initial upstream-thin release.
selections.reason: The Android execution environment and available start methods were inadequate in Epoch 2 evidence.

selections.decision: Adopt external/system-interpreter consumption first and pursue Astral-like managed catalog compatibility only if the exact consumer accepts the truthful Android PYTHON.json and partial build_info.

Generate after install_only. Publish a distinct stripped archive only when a recorded safe strip operation changes bytes; otherwise publish an explicit producer-already-stripped alias identity or omit the duplicate asset according to the release index contract.
selections.release_gate: E3-X4 section census, strip safety, and identity decision

selections.decision: Do not publish symbols or debug products absent from the official producer. Do not create a separate project test artifact; test-material retention is governed by Astral golden projection rules.
selections.reason: No upstream debug/symbol producer bytes exist.

selections.decision: Use the exact official Python.org 3.14.6 aarch64-linux-android archive with its BeeWare-derived dependency closure and minimum API 24. Do not use the API-36 source-equivalent experiment as a production input.
Set the intended product floor to Android API 24 because that is the official input floor; publication at API 24 requires direct qualification and remains withheld until then.
Preserve and statically verify 16 KiB ELF alignment in every transformation; claim actual 16 KiB runtime support only after a real 16 KiB device passes.

The product is a native CLI distribution. Termux app-process and a non-Termux shell/app-private native context are qualification targets; APK/JNI packaging is a separate consumer layer and not part of the archive product.

Track official Python.org Android patch releases, independently version CA/timezone payloads, retain rollback and revocation lineage, and separate upstream native security ownership from project adaptation ownership.
selections.release_gate: E3-G8 patch-update rehearsal and operations contract
